title: Global Clinical Trials Market to Reach $176.32 Billion by 2030 as AI and Decentralized Research Accelerate Growth
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global clinical trials market is projected to grow from $127.16 billion in 2025 to $176.32 billion by 2030, with a CAGR of 6.8%. Growth is driven by AI-enabled trial design, decentralized and virtual trials, precision medicine, and real-world evidence studies. North America leads the market while Asia-Pacific is the fastest-growing region. Strategic consolidation continues with major acquisitions strengthening end-to-end capabilities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a prominent clinical trials market participant in a growing market projected to reach $176.32B by 2030 with 6.8% CAGR, indicating strong industry tailwinds and market opportunity.
Featured as a prominent market participant in a growing clinical trials sector benefiting from increased pharmaceutical R&D investment and outsourcing trends.
Acquired Clario for up to $9.4 billion in October 2025, expanding clinical trial research and data management capabilities, demonstrating strategic investment in the growing market.
Launched One Home for Sites platform in June 2024 supporting decentralized and hybrid trials, positioning the company at the forefront of technology-driven market trends.
Identified as a leading CRO participant in the expanding clinical trials market, with Asia-Pacific expected to be the fastest-growing region through 2030, positioning the company favorably.
Listed among leading clinical trials market participants in an industry experiencing strong growth driven by AI adoption, decentralized trials, and increased CRO outsourcing.

title: Global Cell and Gene Therapy CDMO Market Anticipating Remarkable Growth at a CAGR of ~24% by 2034 | DelveInsight
author: Delveinsight
description: The cell and gene therapy CDMO market is projected to grow from USD 8.3 billion in 2025 to USD 57.1 billion by 2034, driven by increasing clinical development of advanced therapies, rising outsourcing trends among biopharma companies, and technological advancements in manufacturing. North America leads the market with 40% share, while Asia-Pacific emerges as a significant growth hub. Key players are expanding manufacturing capabilities through strategic partnerships.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Expanded commercial manufacturing agreement in March 2026 for gene therapy production, reflecting increasing commercial demand and strong market presence in gene therapy manufacturing services.
Listed as a key company in the growing CDMO market with established capabilities in cell and gene therapy manufacturing, positioned to benefit from market expansion.
Identified as a key player in the cell and gene therapy CDMO market with established manufacturing and testing capabilities, well-positioned for market growth.
Listed among leading CDMO companies and positioned as a strategic hub in Asia-Pacific region with cost-effective operations and skilled talent pool.

title: Cell Banking Outsourcing Market: 16%+ CAGR (2026-2031), Expanding with Rising Demand for Biologics and Regenerative Medicine Services, Says Mordor Intelligence
author: Mordor Intelligence
description: The cell banking outsourcing market is projected to grow from USD 16.78 billion in 2025 to USD 42.31 billion by 2031, with a CAGR of 16.71%. Growth is driven by increasing demand for biologics, cell and gene therapies, and regenerative medicine. North America leads the market while Asia-Pacific emerges as a high-growth region. Biopharmaceutical companies are increasingly outsourcing cell banking operations to improve efficiency and reduce costs.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a major company in the rapidly growing cell banking outsourcing market with 16.71% CAGR through 2031, indicating strong market tailwinds and expansion opportunities for established service providers.
Identified as a major player in the cell banking outsourcing market, positioned to benefit from growing outsourcing activities among pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies.

Listed as a major company in the expanding cell banking outsourcing market, well-positioned to capture growth from increasing demand for specialized cell banking infrastructure and regulatory compliance services.
Mentioned as a major market participant in cell banking outsourcing, positioned to benefit from rising demand for GMP-compliant facilities and quality control services in biologics manufacturing.

title: Global Plasmid DNA Manufacturing Market Anticipates Impressive Growth Trajectory at a CAGR of ~18% by 2034 | DelveInsight
author: Delveinsight
description: The plasmid DNA manufacturing market is projected to grow from USD 2.4 billion in 2025 to USD 10.8 billion by 2034, driven by rising demand for gene therapies, DNA vaccines, and CAR-T treatments. North America leads the market with 44.12% share, while Asia-Pacific emerges as a significant growth engine. Major companies are expanding GMP-compliant manufacturing capabilities to meet increasing demand from biopharmaceutical companies and CDMOs.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key company in the market and specifically mentioned for expanding plasmid DNA manufacturing capacity in the United States with enhanced bioreactors and automated purification systems to support growing demand.
Identified as a leading plasmid DNA manufacturing company with subsidiary Cobra Biologics also mentioned, positioning the company to benefit from market growth.
Listed among key companies in the plasmid DNA manufacturing market, well-positioned to capture growth in gene and cell therapy manufacturing.
Specifically mentioned for announcing continued expansion of GMP plasmid DNA manufacturing capabilities in December 2025 to support growing gene and cell therapy demand.
Listed among key companies in the market with presence in Asia-Pacific, a region identified as a significant growth engine.

title: Global AAV Vector Manufacturing Market Gearing Up for Outstanding Expansion at a CAGR of ~22% by 2034 | DelveInsight
author: Delveinsight
description: The AAV vector manufacturing market is projected to grow from USD 874 million in 2025 to USD 5.2 billion by 2034, driven by expanding gene therapy research, increasing regulatory approvals, and rising investments in biomanufacturing infrastructure. North America leads the market with 43% share, while Asia-Pacific emerges as a key growth engine. Key drivers include growing prevalence of genetic disorders, technological advancements in manufacturing, and expansion of outsourcing to CDMOs.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key company in the rapidly expanding AAV vector manufacturing market with strong growth projections at 22% CAGR through 2034.
Key CDMO player in the AAV manufacturing market, benefiting from outsourcing trends and rising demand for contract manufacturing services.
